pattern: With at most 10 points, test every pair of x-values against every pair of y-values as a candidate rectangle and confirm no stray point sits on or inside it.
hints: A valid rectangle's four corners all come from the given points, so only look at pairs of distinct x-coordinates crossed with pairs of distinct y-coordinates.
For each candidate rectangle, first check whether all four corners actually exist among the points before doing anything else.
Then scan every point once more to make sure none of them (other than the four corners) falls inside the rectangle or touches one of its edges.

edge_inputs.description: fewer than four points, no rectangle possible
exactly the four corners of a square
a fifth point sits exactly on the border of the only candidate rectangle
a fifth point sits strictly inside the only candidate rectangle
points at the extreme coordinate values
two overlapping candidate rectangles of different sizes share corners

stress.description: the maximum ten points scattered over a small grid
